question_text: How is average speed different from instantaneous speed?

options.label: A
options.text: Average speed is the total distance divided by total time; instantaneous speed is the speed at one moment

options.label: B
options.text: They are exactly the same thing

options.label: D
options.text: Average speed only works for cars

correct_answer: Average speed is the total distance divided by total time; instantaneous speed is the speed at one moment
explanation: Average speed tells you the overall rate of travel for the whole journey (total distance ÷ total time). Instantaneous speed is what you measure at a specific moment in time - like what a car's speedometer shows right now. For example, on a car journey, your average speed might be 50 mph for the whole trip, but your instantaneous speed changes constantly as you speed up and slow down.
